Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for Hana Kim's reconciliation findings using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "reconciliation" or "surcharge" or "Hana", filter_in_channel: "C005"). Find Hana's message in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776366000.000000, thread_ts 1776280500.000000) replying to Alejandro's Q3 vendor-cost exposure model post. Hana flags that Swift Relocations retroactively applied a $500 "DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharge" to the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Columbus OH to Seattle WA). The surcharge was not on the original Greg Pallone quote, the bill posted to QuickBooks is $500 higher than communicated, and client invoice INV-2026-0411 still reflects original pricing MoveOps is absorbing $500. Hana suspects the Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy and Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies hazmat moves may also be affected, potentially $1,500 in unbooked vendor costs.
OE 2: Read the full #finance thread using conversations_replies (channel_id: "C005", thread_ts: "1776280500.000000") to get Alejandro's original vendor-cost exposure model post and Hana's reply in full context. Alejandro's original post describes directional risk estimates from the Heartland and Swift rate notices. Hana's reply flags the retroactive DOT surcharge on the Tanaka move and recommends checking the other two hazmat moves.
OE 3: Search emails for Greg Pallone's confirmation of the retroactive DOT surcharges using search_emails (query: "DOT surcharge" or "Greg Pallone" or "Swift revised invoices" or "hazmat"). Find email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com (CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com), email_id "email_swift_dot_surcharge_retroactive", subject "Re: DOT Documentation Surcharges Revised Invoices for MoveOps Hazmat Shipments". Greg confirms the $500 surcharge was applied to all three hazmat shipments: (1) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, (2) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy Swift job #SR-2026-04-1412, (3) Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies Swift job #SR-2026-04-1603. Total additional: $1,500. Surcharges described as non-negotiable.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the Swift retroactive surcharge bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Swift" or DocNumber "SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001"). Find bill Id "SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001" from Swift Relocations (VendorRef: vendor_swift), TotalAmt $1,500.00, Balance $1,500.00, TxnDate 2026-04-24, DueDate 2026-05-10, with three $500 line items one for each hazmat move (Tanaka/Mosaic, Zhao/GreenStack, Patel/NorthWind). Confirm these vendor costs are booked in QuickBooks but not reconciled against client invoices.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for the three affected client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching DocNumber "INV-2026-0411" or CustomerRef for Mosaic). Repeat for INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack) and INV-2026-0412 (NorthWind). Confirm: (1) INV-2026-0411 Mosaic Robotics $9,010, no DOT surcharge line; (2) INV-2026-0418 GreenStack Energy $9,440, no DOT surcharge line; (3) INV-2026-0412 NorthWind Technologies $14,700, no DOT surcharge line. None include the $500 surcharge total unrecovered vendor cost: $1,500.
OE 6: Search CRM for GreenStack Energy engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack") to find the company record (company_greenstack), then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). Find Alejandro's stale margin note (engagement_id: "engagement_greenstack_vendor_margin_note_042026", type: NOTE, dated 2026-04-14) titled "Vendor Cost Check GreenStack Energy April Relocations (Zhao + Reyes)." The note shows Mei-Lin Zhao margin at ~38% based on the original Swift quote of $4,450 and concludes "No action needed for the memo unless vendor rate changes apply retroactively, which I do not expect." This is now stale the $500 DOT surcharge applied retroactively, eroding the margin.
OE 7: Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") to find the company record (company_mosaic), then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]). Find Alejandro's stale vendor cost snapshot (engagement_id: "engagement_mosaic_vendor_cost_confirmed_042026", type: NOTE, dated 2026-04-14) titled "Vendor Cost Snapshot Mosaic Robotics April Batch (3 relocations)." The note states Keiko Tanaka "Vendor costs confirmed at original quote level no changes expected" with a blended margin around 35%. This is now stale the $500 DOT surcharge on the Tanaka move was not reflected.
OE 8: Search Airtable for the Keiko Tanaka relocation record by first discovering the base using airtable_list_bases (find appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Keiko Tanaka"). Find record recKeikoTanakaVendorCostNote in table tblRelocations01 with Special Requirements containing "VENDOR COST NOTE (added April 14 by Alejandro): Swift vendor pricing locked at original quote per Greg Pallone confirmation. Hazmat handling surcharge ($800) included in original scope. No additional charges expected. This move is margin-safe for the strategy memo." This note is stale the $500 retroactive DOT surcharge contradicts the "locked at original quote" and "margin-safe" claims.
OE 9: Search Airtable for the NorthWind Technologies Client Accounts record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "NorthWind"). Find record recAcctNorthWindMarginNote in table tblClientAccts01 with Notes containing "MARGIN NOTE (April 14, Alejandro): Raj Patel move vendor costs are elevated due to hazmat handling and chain-of-custody requirements but within original Swift quote... No further vendor cost increases anticipated on these moves." This note is stale the $500 DOT surcharge on the Raj Patel move was not accounted for and the "no further vendor cost increases anticipated" claim is wrong.
OE 10: Update the stale Keiko Tanaka Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recKeikoTanakaVendorCostNote", fields: {"Special Requirements": corrected text removing the "locked at original quote" and "margin-safe" claims, adding that a $500 DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharge was retroactively applied by Swift per bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001, and the move is no longer margin-safe at the previously stated level}}]).
OE 11: Update the stale NorthWind Technologies Airtable Client Accounts record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcctNorthWindMarginNote", fields: {"Notes": corrected text removing the "No further vendor cost increases anticipated" claim, adding that a $500 DOT surcharge was retroactively applied to the Raj Patel move by Swift, reducing the blended margin below the previously estimated ~28%}}]).
OE 12: Create a corrective CRM engagement note for GreenStack Energy using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: corrective note superseding engagement_greenstack_vendor_margin_note_042026, stating that the Mei-Lin Zhao margin is now lower than the ~38% stated on April 14 due to a $500 retroactive DOT surcharge from Swift on the hazmat move the prior note's assumption that surcharges would not apply retroactively was wrong, company_ids: ["company_greenstack"], title: "CORRECTED Vendor Cost GreenStack Energy (Zhao DOT Surcharge)").
OE 13: Create a corrective CRM engagement note for Mosaic Robotics using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: corrective note superseding engagement_mosaic_vendor_cost_confirmed_042026, stating that Keiko Tanaka vendor costs are NOT confirmed at original quote level a $500 retroactive DOT surcharge was applied by Swift, the prior "no changes expected" claim is wrong, and the blended margin for Mosaic is lower than the ~35% previously stated, company_ids: ["company_mosaic"], title: "CORRECTED Vendor Cost Snapshot Mosaic Robotics (Tanaka DOT Surcharge)").
OE 14: Create a Linear issue to track the vendor cost gap using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" or "Engineering", title: describing the $1,500 unbooked DOT surcharge gap across 3 hazmat shipments, description: covering root cause Swift Relocations retroactively applied $500 DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharges to Tanaka/Mosaic, Zhao/GreenStack, and Patel/NorthWind; QuickBooks bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001 exists but none of the three client invoices (INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0418, INV-2026-0412) include the surcharge; stale margin notes in CRM and Airtable have been corrected; open follow-ups include AM notification, client pass-through decision, and invoice reconciliation process fix, priority: 2 or higher).
OE 15: Post corrected margin analysis to #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload: corrected vendor cost and margin analysis covering all three affected clients Mosaic Robotics/Keiko Tanaka (INV-2026-0411, $500 margin erosion), GreenStack Energy/Mei-Lin Zhao (INV-2026-0418, $500 margin erosion), NorthWind Technologies/Raj Patel (INV-2026-0412, $500 margin erosion) total $1,500 unrecovered vendor cost from Swift bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001, noting that Alejandro's prior margin notes are superseded and these corrected numbers should be used for the pricing meeting and Marcus's strategy memo to Elena). Optionally post as a reply to the existing thread (thread_ts: "1776280500.000000") for context continuity.
OE 16: Look up Alejandro Fuentes's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes"). Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com. Send comprehensive email to Alejandro using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: ["alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com"], subject: covering the Swift DOT surcharge discovery and corrected margins, content: full summary of Hana's finding, Greg Pallone's confirmation email, the three affected clients/moves with dollar-level impact ($500 each, $1,500 total), which specific margin notes were stale and have been corrected (CRM engagement notes for GreenStack and Mosaic, Airtable records for Keiko Tanaka and NorthWind), the corrective actions taken (Airtable updates, CRM corrective notes, Linear ticket, #finance Slack post), and remaining decisions whether to pass through the $500 surcharges to clients or absorb them, and that Marcus should not use the old numbers in front of Elena).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for Hana Kim's reconciliation findings using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "reconciliation" or "surcharge" or "Hana", filter_in_channel: "C005"). Find Hana's message in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776366000.000000, thread_ts 1776280500.000000) replying to Alejandro's Q3 vendor-cost exposure model post. Hana flags that Swift Relocations retroactively applied a $500 "DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharge" to the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Columbus OH to Seattle WA). The surcharge was not on the original Greg Pallone quote, the bill posted to QuickBooks is $500 higher than communicated, and client invoice INV-2026-0411 still reflects original pricing MoveOps is absorbing $500. Hana suspects the Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy and Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies hazmat moves may also be affected, potentially $1,500 in unbooked vendor costs.
OE 2: Read the full #finance thread using conversations_replies (channel_id: "C005", thread_ts: "1776280500.000000") to get Alejandro's original vendor-cost exposure model post and Hana's reply in full context. Alejandro's original post describes directional risk estimates from the Heartland and Swift rate notices. Hana's reply flags the retroactive DOT surcharge on the Tanaka move and recommends checking the other two hazmat moves.
OE 3: Search emails for Greg Pallone's confirmation of the retroactive DOT surcharges using search_emails (query: "DOT surcharge" or "Greg Pallone" or "Swift revised invoices" or "hazmat"). Find email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com (CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com), email_id "email_swift_dot_surcharge_retroactive", subject "Re: DOT Documentation Surcharges Revised Invoices for MoveOps Hazmat Shipments". Greg confirms the $500 surcharge was applied to all three hazmat shipments: (1) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, (2) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy Swift job #SR-2026-04-1412, (3) Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies Swift job #SR-2026-04-1603. Total additional: $1,500. Surcharges described as non-negotiable.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the Swift retroactive surcharge bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Swift" or DocNumber "SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001"). Find bill Id "SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001" from Swift Relocations (VendorRef: vendor_swift), TotalAmt $1,500.00, Balance $1,500.00, TxnDate 2026-04-24, DueDate 2026-05-10, with three $500 line items one for each hazmat move (Tanaka/Mosaic, Zhao/GreenStack, Patel/NorthWind). Confirm these vendor costs are booked in QuickBooks but not reconciled against client invoices.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for the three affected client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching DocNumber "INV-2026-0411" or CustomerRef for Mosaic). Repeat for INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack) and INV-2026-0412 (NorthWind). Confirm: (1) INV-2026-0411 Mosaic Robotics $9,010, no DOT surcharge line; (2) INV-2026-0418 GreenStack Energy $9,440, no DOT surcharge line; (3) INV-2026-0412 NorthWind Technologies $14,700, no DOT surcharge line. None include the $500 surcharge total unrecovered vendor cost: $1,500.
OE 6: Search CRM for GreenStack Energy engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack") to find the company record (company_greenstack), then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). Find Alejandro's stale margin note (engagement_id: "engagement_greenstack_vendor_margin_note_042026", type: NOTE, dated 2026-04-14) titled "Vendor Cost Check GreenStack Energy April Relocations (Zhao + Reyes)." The note shows Mei-Lin Zhao margin at ~38% based on the original Swift quote of $4,450 and concludes "No action needed for the memo unless vendor rate changes apply retroactively, which I do not expect." This is now stale the $500 DOT surcharge applied retroactively, eroding the margin.
OE 7: Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") to find the company record (company_mosaic), then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]). Find Alejandro's stale vendor cost snapshot (engagement_id: "engagement_mosaic_vendor_cost_confirmed_042026", type: NOTE, dated 2026-04-14) titled "Vendor Cost Snapshot Mosaic Robotics April Batch (3 relocations)." The note states Keiko Tanaka "Vendor costs confirmed at original quote level no changes expected" with a blended margin around 35%. This is now stale the $500 DOT surcharge on the Tanaka move was not reflected.
OE 8: Search Airtable for the Keiko Tanaka relocation record by first discovering the base using airtable_list_bases (find appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Keiko Tanaka"). Find record recKeikoTanakaVendorCostNote in table tblRelocations01 with Special Requirements containing "VENDOR COST NOTE (added April 14 by Alejandro): Swift vendor pricing locked at original quote per Greg Pallone confirmation. Hazmat handling surcharge ($800) included in original scope. No additional charges expected. This move is margin-safe for the strategy memo." This note is stale the $500 retroactive DOT surcharge contradicts the "locked at original quote" and "margin-safe" claims.
OE 9: Search Airtable for the NorthWind Technologies Client Accounts record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "NorthWind"). Find record recAcctNorthWindMarginNote in table tblClientAccts01 with Notes containing "MARGIN NOTE (April 14, Alejandro): Raj Patel move vendor costs are elevated due to hazmat handling and chain-of-custody requirements but within original Swift quote... No further vendor cost increases anticipated on these moves." This note is stale the $500 DOT surcharge on the Raj Patel move was not accounted for and the "no further vendor cost increases anticipated" claim is wrong.
OE 10: Update the stale Keiko Tanaka Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recKeikoTanakaVendorCostNote", fields: {"Special Requirements": corrected text removing the "locked at original quote" and "margin-safe" claims, adding that a $500 DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharge was retroactively applied by Swift per bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001, and the move is no longer margin-safe at the previously stated level}}]).
OE 11: Update the stale NorthWind Technologies Airtable Client Accounts record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcctNorthWindMarginNote", fields: {"Notes": corrected text removing the "No further vendor cost increases anticipated" claim, adding that a $500 DOT surcharge was retroactively applied to the Raj Patel move by Swift, reducing the blended margin below the previously estimated ~28%}}]).
OE 12: Create a corrective CRM engagement note for GreenStack Energy using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: corrective note superseding engagement_greenstack_vendor_margin_note_042026, stating that the Mei-Lin Zhao margin is now lower than the ~38% stated on April 14 due to a $500 retroactive DOT surcharge from Swift on the hazmat move the prior note's assumption that surcharges would not apply retroactively was wrong, company_ids: ["company_greenstack"], title: "CORRECTED Vendor Cost GreenStack Energy (Zhao DOT Surcharge)").
OE 13: Create a corrective CRM engagement note for Mosaic Robotics using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: corrective note superseding engagement_mosaic_vendor_cost_confirmed_042026, stating that Keiko Tanaka vendor costs are NOT confirmed at original quote level a $500 retroactive DOT surcharge was applied by Swift, the prior "no changes expected" claim is wrong, and the blended margin for Mosaic is lower than the ~35% previously stated, company_ids: ["company_mosaic"], title: "CORRECTED Vendor Cost Snapshot Mosaic Robotics (Tanaka DOT Surcharge)").
OE 14: Create a Linear issue to track the vendor cost gap using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" or "Engineering", title: describing the $1,500 unbooked DOT surcharge gap across 3 hazmat shipments, description: covering root cause Swift Relocations retroactively applied $500 DOT Compliance Documentation Surcharges to Tanaka/Mosaic, Zhao/GreenStack, and Patel/NorthWind; QuickBooks bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001 exists but none of the three client invoices (INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0418, INV-2026-0412) include the surcharge; stale margin notes in CRM and Airtable have been corrected; open follow-ups include AM notification, client pass-through decision, and invoice reconciliation process fix, priority: 2 or higher).
OE 15: Post corrected margin analysis to #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload: corrected vendor cost and margin analysis covering all three affected clients Mosaic Robotics/Keiko Tanaka (INV-2026-0411, $500 margin erosion), GreenStack Energy/Mei-Lin Zhao (INV-2026-0418, $500 margin erosion), NorthWind Technologies/Raj Patel (INV-2026-0412, $500 margin erosion) total $1,500 unrecovered vendor cost from Swift bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001, noting that Alejandro's prior margin notes are superseded and these corrected numbers should be used for the pricing meeting and Marcus's strategy memo to Elena). Optionally post as a reply to the existing thread (thread_ts: "1776280500.000000") for context continuity.
OE 16: Look up Alejandro Fuentes's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes"). Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com. Send comprehensive email to Alejandro using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: ["alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com"], subject: covering the Swift DOT surcharge discovery and corrected margins, content: full summary of Hana's finding, Greg Pallone's confirmation email, the three affected clients/moves with dollar-level impact ($500 each, $1,500 total), which specific margin notes were stale and have been corrected (CRM engagement notes for GreenStack and Mosaic, Airtable records for Keiko Tanaka and NorthWind), the corrective actions taken (Airtable updates, CRM corrective notes, Linear ticket, #finance Slack post), and remaining decisions whether to pass through the $500 surcharges to clients or absorb them, and that Marcus should not use the old numbers in front of Elena).

It does not mention corrected CRM engagement notes for GreenStack or Mosaic (which were never created), nor does it reference any corrected NorthWind Airtable record. 21 The email to [alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com](mailto:alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) (visible in send_email parameters) warns that Marcus should not use the old margin numbers in front of Elena (or similar warning about stale data).
outcome

✓Run #2The email explicitly ends with 'Get with Marcus before the Wednesday memo goes to Elena.' This directly warns about stale data being used in front of Elena.
✓Run #3The email explicitly states 'If Marcus or Elena uses those numbers in the vendor strategy memo without correction, they'll be working with stale data,' directly warning about the risk of Marcus using bad numbers in front of Elena.
✓Run #4The email explicitly asks 'Do any of these corrected numbers need to reach Elena before her next strategy discussion with Marcus?' and the final response states 'That call needs to happen before any of these numbers go in front of Elena,' clearly warning about stale data risk.
✓Run #5The email opens with 'Putting this in writing so you have the full picture in one place before Marcus takes anything to Elena,' which explicitly warns against using stale numbers in front of Elena.
✗Run #6The email mentions Marcus in the context of briefing him before the pricing meeting and walking back the 'numbers are solid' assessment, but never explicitly mentions Elena or warns that Marcus should not use old numbers in front of Elena. The Slack post asks 'Does this change any of the numbers going in front of Elena?' but the email does not include this warning.
22 The agent's final response to the user identifies that the total unrecovered vendor cost from Swift's retroactive DOT surcharges is $1,500 across the 3 hazmat shipments.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent's final response explicitly states 'Total understatement: $1,500 across 3 clients' and identifies the three $500 surcharges via the SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001 bill.
✓Run #2The agent's final response clearly states 'Bill SR-2026-DOT-RETRO-001 (posted April 24, due May 10) confirms all three surcharges — $1,500 total' with a table showing $500 for each of the three moves.
✓Run #3The agent's final response states '$1,500 in vendor costs were sitting in payables' and the table shows three $500 entries summing to $1,500. The final response summary clearly states the total unrecovered vendor cost is $1,500.
✓Run #4The agent's final response explicitly states 'Impact: $1,500 in Unbooked Vendor Costs' and 'None of these surcharges are on the client invoices. MoveOps is currently absorbing the full $1,500'.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'The $1,500 Gap — Confirmed in QuickBooks' and 'MoveOps is absorbing the full $1,500,' clearly identifying the total unrecovered vendor cost.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states '$1,500 total' unbooked surcharge across the three hazmat moves and includes a table showing $500 per client summing to $1,500, with 'MoveOps is absorbing all $1,500'.
23 The email to [alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com](mailto:alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) (visible in send_email parameters) or the agent's final response identifies that none of the three client invoices (INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic at $9,010, INV-2026-0418 for GreenStack at $9,440, INV-2026-0412 for NorthWind at $14,700) include the $500 DOT surcharge meaning MoveOps is currently absorbing the cost.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both the email and the final response state that the surcharges were 'not passed through to any client invoice' and that MoveOps was 'silently absorbing $1,500'. The three specific invoice amounts and numbers are listed along with the unbooked surcharge per move.
✓Run #2The final response states 'None of the client invoices include this surcharge — MoveOps is absorbing all $1,500 without approval.' The email also states 'NONE of the corresponding client invoices include this surcharge. We are absorbing all $1,500 right now.'
✓Run #3The email states '$1,500 in vendor costs were sitting in payables with no corresponding client pass-through and no internal approval to absorb them' and specifically notes 'we have NOT passed this through to the client invoice' for each move, making clear MoveOps is absorbing the cost.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states 'Total unbooked vendor cost: $1,500 / Current status: MoveOps absorbing all $1,500 — no pass-through to any client yet' and lists each invoice (INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0418, INV-2026-0412) noting none includes the DOT surcharge. The final response also states this.
✓Run #5Both the final response ('None of these surcharges are reflected on client invoices. MoveOps is absorbing the full $1,500') and the email content confirm that the surcharges exist in QuickBooks but have not been passed through to client invoices.
✓Run #6Both the email and the final response explicitly state that none of the three client invoices (INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0418, INV-2026-0412) reflect the DOT surcharge and that 'MoveOps is absorbing all $1,500' / 'MoveOps is absorbing this cost with no internal approval'.

query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #4The first conversations_search_messages call used query 'Hana reconciliation' which contains both 'Hana' and 'reconciliation' — matching the criterion's allowed terms. A subsequent call also used 'Hana' alone.
✓Run #5The very first conversations_search_messages call used search_query 'Hana reconciliation,' which contains both 'Hana' and 'reconciliation' — directly matching the criterion's required terms. This successfully surfaced Hana Kim's message about the retroactive DOT surcharge.
✓Run #6The first conversations_search_messages call used search_query 'reconciliation' with filter_users_from 'Hana', which directly matches the criterion's requirement for a query containing 'reconciliation' or 'Hana'.

✗Run #3The two search_emails calls used queries 'Hana reconciliation' and 'margin notes pricing'. Neither contains 'DOT surcharge', 'Greg Pallone', 'Swift', 'hazmat', or 'revised invoices' as required by this criterion.

None of these mention 'DOT surcharge', 'Greg Pallone', 'Swift', 'hazmat', or 'revised invoices' as required by this criterion.
